title: leptospermum citratum
intro: Leptospermum citratum, also known as the Lemon Tea Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, native to Australia. The plant's leaves and flowers contain compounds with antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties. The health benefits of Leptospermum citratum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies. Leptospermum citratum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, essential oil, and extracts, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
